title: castalia minor
intro: Castalia minor, also known as Barmsley or Castalia, is a small, aquatic plant that belongs to the Arum family. Traditionally, Castalia minor has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. The plant is rich in saponins, which contribute to its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects, helping to alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and promoting overall well-being. Castalia minor can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various ailments.
